---
title: "Education"
---

You can download my CV here:

[Download CV](files/Li_Yuan_CV.pdf)

# Schools

**PhD in Statistical Science**  
Southern Methodist University, Dallas, Texas, United States

2022 – present

- **Ph.D. Qualifying Exam / Candidacy Milestone:** Passed the qualifying examination, also referred to in the department letter as the Basic Exam, and was confirmed eligible to proceed in the Statistics PhD program. The Department of Statistical Science also selected me for the Scheuren Award for outstanding performance on the theory exam.  
  [View official congratulations letter](files/smu_phd_qualifying_exam_congratulations_letter.pdf){.btn .btn-primary}
  [View qualifying exam research paper](files/Phd_qualifying.pdf){.btn .btn-secondary}

  Summary: This paper synthesizes research on statistical text analysis, attention-based neural networks, and counterfactually augmented data for sentiment inference. It also reproduces computational results from an attention-based multiple-instance classification model.

**MS in Statistical Science**  
Southern Methodist University, Dallas, Texas, United States

2022 – 2025

**MS in Data Science**  
Vanderbilt University, Nashville, Tennessee, United States

2020 – 2022

**Berkeley Global Access Program (BGA) - Multidisciplinary**  
University of California, Berkeley, California, United States

2019 – 2020

**BS in Statistics**  
Sichuan University, Chengdu, Sichuan, China

2015 – 2019

# Technical Skills

- Programming: Python, R, C, SQL, LaTeX, C++, JavaScript, HTML/CSS
- Statistical modeling: Bayesian modeling, time-series analysis, regression, machine learning, deep learning
- Tools: Git, GitHub, VS Code, Quarto, LaTeX, Jupyter Notebook, RStudio, PyCharm, Tableau, Microsoft Office
- Data analysis: Data cleaning, exploratory data analysis, statistical inference, visualization
- Natural language processing: Text preprocessing, sentiment analysis, text classification, topic modeling
